name: Beef and Stout Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Sauté the beef and bacon in a little oil until the beef is browned.
2. Drain off any excess fat from the pan.
3. Remove the meat and set aside.
4. Add butter to the pan, melt it, then stir in the flour to make a roux.
5. Gradually add the stout while stirring continuously.
6. Place the meat and small onions (peeled) in a deep casserole dish.
7. Season with salt, pepper, basil, parsley, and sprinkle sugar on top.
8. Pour in the sauce made from wine or cider vinegar.
9. Cover and place in an oven preheated to 300°F.
10. Cook gently for up to 3 hours, checking occasionally.
11. If needed, add more stout to prevent drying out.
12. Remove from the oven and stir in the vinegar before serving with boiled potatoes.
